content.full_text:
October 22, 1991

Mr. Scott Hallbrin  
Acting General Counsel, Legal Division  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Hallbrin:

Enron Corp. is currently registered in California as a Lobbyist Employer and retains the lobbying firm of Applied Strategies. Enron Corp. is the parent corporation of numerous subsidiaries. Applied Strategies lobbies in California on behalf of several Enron subsidiaries, including Enron Liquid Fuels, Enron Gas Marketing, Enron Oil & Gas, Enron Power Corp., and Transwestern Pipeline Company. Per the contract with Applied Strategies, Enron Corp. pays Applied Strategies with one check and then allocates respective portions of that expense to the appropriate Enron subsidiaries.

We would like confirmation from you that registration of Enron Corp., the parent corporation, is sufficient to cover lobbying activities performed for the benefit of Enron subsidiaries. Obviously, we would prefer to have a single registration cover lobbying activities performed for all Enron subsidiaries rather than having to register for each individual subsidiary. Ms. Mary Ann Kvasager, who is in the technical assistance division at FPPC, suggested an amendment to our registration reflecting, "Enron Corp. and all subsidiaries" or "Enron Corp. and subsidiaries including ..." to eliminate excessive filings and the need to register all subsidiaries separately.

We would appreciate a letter from you confirming that we fully comply with FPPC requirements on behalf of Enron and its subsidiaries and/or the need to file the suggested amendment.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

Nancy L. Vandenberg  
Director  
State Government Affairs

NV/gw

cc: Richard E. Ratcliff, Applied strategies  
    Elizabeth Labanowski
